usage_policy: Rules are original synthesis. Source IDs identify upstream evidence and established practice; do not reproduce upstream passages, code, icons, or assets without checking their current terms.

sources.title: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2
sources.license_note: Use under current W3C document terms; this corpus contains original synthesis, not copied normative text.
sources.summary: Normative accessibility success criteria and conformance model.
when_to_use: Any interface that must be perceivable, operable, understandable, or robust.

sources.title: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
sources.license_note: Use under current W3C terms; verify the live pattern and support notes before implementation.
sources.summary: Authoring practices for ARIA patterns, keyboard interaction, and widget structure.
when_to_use: Building or auditing complex widgets and keyboard UX.

sources.title: MDN Web Docs
sources.license_note: Consult MDN's current attribution and licensing terms; this corpus paraphrases platform behavior.
sources.summary: Platform reference for HTML/CSS/JS behavior, accessibility, and compatibility.
when_to_use: Need authoritative platform behavior rather than framework opinion.

sources.title: web.dev
sources.license_note: Consult the current site terms; this corpus synthesizes concepts without reproducing articles.
sources.summary: Chrome-team guidance on performance, responsive design, forms, and web quality.
when_to_use: Optimizing delivery, rendering, and modern web quality.

sources.title: React documentation
sources.license_note: Consult the documentation repository's current license and attribution terms.
sources.summary: Official React documentation for component architecture and state behavior.
when_to_use: React-specific implementation decisions.

sources.title: Next.js documentation
sources.license_note: Consult the documentation repository's current license and attribution terms.
sources.summary: Official Next.js docs for App Router, rendering, metadata, and deployment patterns.
when_to_use: Working in a Next.js codebase.

sources.title: Material Design 3 and Material Web
sources.source_type: Official design system and implementation repository
sources.license_note: Material Web Apache-2.0; Material site/brand assets subject to Google terms
sources.summary: Material Design 3 system guidance and related implementation references.
when_to_use: Product already committed to Material language.

sources.title: Apple Human Interface Guidelines
sources.license_note: Use as platform guidance under Apple's current terms; this corpus contains no copied Apple assets.
sources.summary: Apple HIG for clarity, accessibility, motion restraint, and platform craft.
when_to_use: Need platform-grade interaction principles, not Apple asset copying.

sources.title: Fluent 2 Design System
sources.license_note: Fluent UI repository MIT; referenced fonts/icons can have separate asset terms
sources.summary: Microsoft Fluent 2 design system for enterprise product UI and tokens.
when_to_use: Building Fluent-aligned or enterprise Windows-adjacent experiences.

sources.title: Carbon Design System
sources.source_type: Official design system and component repository
sources.license_note: Apache-2.0
sources.summary: IBM Carbon design system for dense enterprise and data products.
when_to_use: Complex operational interfaces needing structured systems.

sources.title: Shopify Polaris
sources.license_note: Guidance is synthesized; product marks and assets may have separate restrictions.
sources.summary: Shopify Polaris guidance for commerce admin and merchant workflows.
when_to_use: Shopify admin/commerce product surfaces.

sources.title: Primer
sources.source_type: Official product and brand design system
sources.license_note: Primer React MIT; brand assets and GitHub marks have separate terms
sources.summary: GitHub Primer system for developer-tool interfaces and practical a11y patterns.
when_to_use: Building tools for technical audiences.

sources.title: GOV.UK Design System
sources.license_note: Guidance is synthesized; consult the site's current licensing and attribution notice for reuse.
sources.summary: GOV.UK system focused on clear public-service forms and evidenced accessibility.
when_to_use: High-stakes informational or government-like services.

sources.title: U.S. Web Design System
sources.publisher: U.S. General Services Administration and contributors
sources.license_note: Guidance is synthesized; verify current federal, third-party, trademark, and repository notices.
sources.summary: U.S. Web Design System for accessible federal/public services.
when_to_use: Civic/public-facing experiences needing clarity and trust.

sources.title: Spectrum
sources.license_note: Spectrum site/brand assets subject to Adobe terms; React Spectrum repository Apache-2.0
sources.summary: Adobe Spectrum system with strong color and adaptive design guidance.
when_to_use: Designing resilient cross-product component systems.

sources.title: React Aria
sources.license_note: Guidance is synthesized; check the current repository license before reusing code.
sources.summary: Accessible React behavior hooks/components across modalities and locales.
when_to_use: Need behavior correctness more than styling.

sources.title: Radix Primitives
sources.license_note: Guidance is synthesized; check the current repository license before reusing code.
sources.summary: Unstyled accessible React primitives used widely under design systems like shadcn.
when_to_use: Need composable accessible behavior with custom visuals.

sources.title: Transitions.dev repository
sources.source_type: GitHub repository, examples, skill, and build generator
sources.license_note: MIT for Refine per terms.html; no root LICENSE was visible, so repository-wide scope is not assumed
sources.summary: Motion transition showcase and generator-oriented repository.
when_to_use: Refining motion language; verify license before copying code.

sources.title: Skills for Design Engineers
sources.license_note: MIT
sources.summary: Practitioner skills for animation review, motion vocabulary, and design craft.
when_to_use: Need opinionated motion craft heuristics with accessibility exceptions.

sources.title: Taste Skill — Anti-Slop Frontend Framework
sources.license_note: MIT
sources.summary: Anti-slop frontend skill collection emphasizing brief inference and completion checks.
when_to_use: Need experimental workflow ideas; do not treat aesthetic bans as universal law.

sources.title: Kill AI Slop
sources.source_type: GitHub field guide, agent skill, and static scanner
sources.license_note: No repository license was stated at revision eb4857a; inspiration-only, with no copied or adapted prose, examples, regexes, or code.
sources.summary: Field guide/scanner taxonomy for detecting AI-slop visual and copy signals.
when_to_use: Inspiration only while license remains unresolved; synthesize, do not copy.

sources.title: Amicro — Micro-transitions
sources.source_type: GitHub React micro-interaction showcase and demo site
sources.license_note: README claims MIT, but no LICENSE file or GitHub license metadata existed at revision 5914ede; inspiration-only, with no copied or adapted code, assets, or brand marks.
sources.summary: React micro-interaction showcase for control-state feedback ideas.
when_to_use: Inspiration only; license claim lacked LICENSE file at inspection.

findability.note: Findability fields help retrieval choose registered evidence sources; provenance remains in the registry.
